title: Add `--cite-coverage` / `--since` / `--client` CLI flags + server runDoctorCiteCoverage entry stub
description: Plumb three new citty args on `fab doctor`: --cite-coverage (boolean, mutually exclusive with --fix/--fix-knowledge), --since (string, default '7d', accepts duration suffix like '7d'/'24h'/'30m' OR epoch-ms string), --client (string, enum 'cc'|'codex'|'cursor'|'all', default 'all'). When --cite-coverage is set, the CLI run dispatcher takes the FAST-PATH branch: skip the 28-check inspection pipeline; instead call new server export runDoctorCiteCoverage(target, options). Server function is a stub returning a CiteCoverageReport shape with marker_ts (from TASK-04) + placeholder metrics — actual algorithm lands in TASK-06.

files.target: DoctorArgs type (L21-L39) + args declarations (L71-L109) + run dispatcher branching (L110-L244)
files.change: (1) Add to DoctorArgs: `'cite-coverage'?: boolean; since?: string; client?: string`. (2) Add args entries: cite-coverage (type:'boolean', description i18n key cli.doctor.args.cite-coverage.description); since (type:'string', default:'7d', description key); client (type:'string', default:'all', valueHint:'cc|codex|cursor|all', description key). (3) In run() body before the existing rescan/inspect dispatch, add early-return branch: if (args['cite-coverage']) { validate mutex with --fix/--fix-knowledge (error if either set); parse --since via parseSinceDuration helper; validate --client enum; dispatch to runDoctorCiteCoverage(target, {since: parsedSinceMs, client: clientFilter}); render via renderCiteCoverageReport (TASK-07 will replace with rich formatter; TASK-05 ships minimal JSON-stringify or single-line print); respect --json; return. }

Add `parseSinceDuration(input)`: accepts 'Nd' (days), 'Nh' (hours), 'Nm' (minutes), or numeric epoch-ms string. Returns epoch-ms floor (Date.now() - deltaMs). Invalid input → throw with i18n error key. Default '7d' = Date.now() - 7*86400_000.

Define `type CiteCoverageReport = { status: 'ok' | 'skipped', marker_ts: number, marker_emitted_now: boolean, since_ts: number, client_filter: 'cc'|'codex'|'cursor'|'all', metrics: { edits_touched: number, qualifying_cites: number, recalled_unverified: number, expected_but_missed: number, total_turns: number }, per_client?: Record<string, Partial<CiteCoverageReport['metrics']>>, dismissed_reason_histogram?: Record<string, number>, generated_at: string }`. Implement runDoctorCiteCoverage(projectRoot, options) STUB: call ensureCitePolicyActivatedMarker → if marker_ts==0 return {status:'skipped', ...zeros, ...}; else return {status:'ok', marker_ts, marker_emitted_now, since_ts: options.since, client_filter: options.client, metrics: zero-filled, generated_at: new Date().toISOString()}. TASK-06 replaces stub with real algorithm.
Add `export { runDoctorCiteCoverage } from './services/doctor'` and `export type { CiteCoverageReport } from './services/doctor'` so CLI can import.

Add: `cli.doctor.args.cite-coverage.description = 'Generate cite policy adherence report (read-only; skips standard inspections)'`, `cli.doctor.args.since.description = 'Time window for cite coverage (e.g. 7d, 24h, 30m)'`, `cli.doctor.args.client.description = 'Filter cite coverage by client (cc|codex|cursor|all)'`, `cli.doctor.errors.cite-coverage-mutex = '--cite-coverage cannot be combined with --fix or --fix-knowledge'`, `cli.doctor.errors.invalid-since = 'Invalid --since value: {input}. Expected duration like 7d, 24h, 30m or epoch ms.'`, `cli.doctor.errors.invalid-client = 'Invalid --client value: {input}. Expected cc, codex, cursor, or all.'`
Add Chinese mirrors of the 6 new keys above (descriptions + 3 error messages).

implementation: Read packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts L21-L244 to confirm existing arg + dispatcher patterns.
Add new args to DoctorArgs type + args block following existing --fix-knowledge pattern.
Implement parseSinceDuration helper near top of file or in a shared util — regex /^(\d+)([dhm])$/ for duration, else parseInt for epoch-ms.
Add early-return cite-coverage branch in run() dispatcher BEFORE the standard inspection pipeline starts.
In server doctor.ts: define CiteCoverageReport type + runDoctorCiteCoverage stub (calls ensureCitePolicyActivatedMarker from TASK-04, returns zero metrics).
Add 6 new i18n keys to en.ts + zh-CN.ts symmetrically.
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli build` + `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server build` to confirm typescript clean.
Run `fab doctor --cite-coverage --since 7d --client all` in a test workspace; expect zero-metric JSON-ish report + marker event in events.jsonl.

reference.pattern: citty arg declaration + run dispatcher early-return branch
reference.examples: Existing --fix / --fix-knowledge / --rescan / --strict / --json args show the pattern verbatim.

rationale.chosen_approach: Flag (not subcommand) per auto-orchestrator Q-3 option 3: maintains doctor's existing flag-only design while fast-path skips 28-check overhead. Stub server function ships in TASK-05 so TASK-06 algorithm can land in isolation without coupling CLI + algorithm in one commit.
alternatives_considered: Subcommand `fab doctor cite-coverage` (rejected: changes doctor's design; user expressed CLI design preference for flat surface)
Land algorithm in TASK-05 (rejected: commits get fat; separation of concerns easier to review)
rationale.tradeoffs: Stub returns zero metrics — TASK-06 must land before report is meaningful. Mitigated by TASK-05's metrics structure already final (TASK-06 only fills in numbers).

manual_checks: Run `fab doctor --cite-coverage` in test workspace; assert exits 0 with stub report + cite_policy_activated event in events.jsonl
Run `fab doctor --cite-coverage --fix` and assert error message about mutex
Run `fab doctor --cite-coverage --since invalid` and assert error message
Run `fab doctor --cite-coverage --client xyz` and assert error
Run `fab doctor --cite-coverage --json` and assert valid JSON output
success_metrics: All 3 new args appear in `fab doctor --help`
Mutex validation throws before any inspection runs
Stub report has correct shape and respects --json
Marker event emitted to events.jsonl on first run
integration: CLI smoke: `fab doctor --cite-coverage --since=1d --client=cc` exits 0 + emits marker

criteria: DoctorArgs type includes cite-coverage, since, client fields
citty args block declares all 3 flags with i18n-backed descriptions
parseSinceDuration correctly handles 7d/24h/30m suffixes + epoch-ms passthrough + invalid throw
run() dispatcher early-returns to runDoctorCiteCoverage when --cite-coverage set (no 28-check pipeline runs)
Mutex with --fix/--fix-knowledge enforced before any I/O
Marker event emitted on first invocation (via ensureCitePolicyActivatedMarker)
CiteCoverageReport type shape matches TASK-06/07 contract (metrics object, per_client, dismissed_reason_histogram)
i18n keys added symmetrically to en.ts + zh-CN.ts
`fab doctor --cite-coverage --json` returns valid JSON; --json absent prints minimal text
Commit msg: `feat(rc20): fab doctor --cite-coverage flag + server entry (TASK-05)`
